prs.title: made home page, did product listing, filtering, sorting
fix: make footer icon links functional in OpenSource page
prs.body: ### Description This PR updates the footer section of the Contribute page to remove non-functional icons and improve user interaction by linking the GitHub repository directly. ### Changes Made - **Added Functionality:** Wrapped the GitHub icon in an external anchor (``) tag pointing to the [`Tushar8466/devtrack`](https://github.com/Tushar8466/devtrack) repository with `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"`. - **Improved UX:** Added smooth scaling and color hover transitions on the GitHub icon for better interactivity. - **Cleaned Up UI:** Removed the non-clickable native Globe and Terminal icons per design request to declutter the footer layout. ### Files Affected - `src/app/contribute/page.tsx` ###Screenshots - Before - After | GitHub, Globe, Terminal icons present but non-functional | GitHub icon links to repo; Globe & Terminal icons removed | | No hover feedback on footer icons | Smooth scale + color transition on GitHub icon hover | ### Testing - [ ] GitHub icon navigates to `https://github.com/Tushar8466/devtrack` in a new tab - [ ] Hover transition animates correctly on the GitHub icon - [ ] Globe and Terminal icons no longer appear in footer - [ ] No layout shifts or regressions on the Contribute page ### Related Issue Closes #6

prs.title: fix: center SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS badge text on Explore page
prs.body:
## Summary
Fixes the misaligned text inside the `SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS` green pill badge on the Explore page. The text was not centered within the container, causing a visual inconsistency.

Closes #<issue-number>

## Root Cause
The green pill badge was missing centering utility classes, causing the text to appear off-center inside the rounded container.

## Changes
- Added `flex items-center justify-center text-center` to the `SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS` badge container in the Explore page component

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes a UI issue)

## Testing
- [ ] Verified `SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS` text is centered inside the green pill on the Explore page
- [ ] No visual regressions on surrounding components
- [ ] Checked on both mobile and desktop viewports

## Screenshots
<!-- Add before/after screenshots -->

prs.title: fix: add cursor-pointer styles to buttons on OpenSource landing page
prs.body:
## Summary
Fixes incorrect cursor styles on the OpenSource Tracker landing page buttons. In Tailwind v4, the default button behavior falls back to the arrow cursor instead of the expected pointer cursor. This PR adds explicit `cursor-pointer` classes to resolve this.

## Changes
- Added `cursor-pointer` to the following buttons in `page.tsx`:
  - **Contribution Tracker**, **Node Intelligence**, **Guide**
  - **Start Contributing**
  - **Intercept Node Telemetry** (on all assets)
  - **Get Started** and **Read Documentation**

## Root Cause
Tailwind v4 changed default button cursor behavior, requiring explicit `cursor-pointer` classes on interactive elements.

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

## Testing
- [ ] Verified buttons show pointer cursor on hover in local dev
- [ ] No visual regressions on the OpenSource landing page

## Screenshots
<!-- Add before/after screenshots if possible -->

issues.title: 🐛 Bug Report: Footer Icon Links Are Non-Functional
issues.body: ### Description The footer of the DevTrack website contains three icon links (GitHub, Website/Globe, and Terminal/CLI) but none of them appear to be clickable or navigate anywhere when clicked. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to the DevTrack homepage 2. Scroll to the bottom of the page (footer section) 3. Observe the three footer icons: GitHub (octocat), Globe (website), and Terminal (CLI) 4. Click on any of the three icons ### Expected Behavior Each icon should link to its respective destination: - 🐙 **GitHub icon** → should navigate to the project's GitHub repository - 🌐 **Globe icon** → should navigate to the project's official website - `>_` **Terminal icon** → should link to CLI docs or relevant resource ### Actual Behavior The icons are present in the footer but clicking them has no effect — they are either missing `href` attributes or the links are broken/empty. ### Environment - **Page:** Footer section (bottom of any page) - **Browser:** [Please add your browser + version] - **OS:** [Please add your OS] ### Screenshots ### Additional Context The footer also displays the tagline: `// NEURAL AUTHORSHIP ESTABLISHED // HUMAN PRIMARY CONTRIBUTOR //` The non-functional links create a poor user experience, especially the GitHub icon which is a key entry point for contributors and visitors looking to explore or fork the project. ### Suggested Fix Ensure each footer icon is wrapped in a proper `` tag with the correct `href`, `target="_blank"`, and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` attributes. ```html ```

issues.title: Bug Report: Text is misaligned
issues.body: ## Description The `SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS` label (green pill/badge) on the Explore page is not centered horizontally. The text appears left-aligned or off-center within the green rounded container, causing a visual inconsistency. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to the **Explore** page 2. Observe the `SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS` green badge at the top 3. Notice the text is not centered within the pill ## Expected Behavior The text `SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS` should be horizontally centered within the green rounded badge/pill. ## Actual Behavior The text appears off-center (not centered) inside the green container. ## Screenshots ## Possible Fix Ensure the container has `text-center` or `flex items-center justify-center` applied in Tailwind, or check for any conflicting padding/margin causing the text shift. ## Environment - Page: Explore - Component: `SEQUENCE_ANALYSIS` badge/pill - Styling: Tailwind CSS

issues.title: [Bug] "Contribution Tracker" button missing pointer cursor on hover
issues.body: Description The "Contribution Tracker" button on the homepage hero section does not display the expected pointer cursor when hovered. The cursor remains the default arrow, which is inconsistent with the other CTA buttons ("Node Intelligence", "Guide") on the same section and breaks standard web UX expectations for interactive elements. Steps to reproduce Navigate to the homepage. Scroll to the "Track Your Contribution Legacy" hero section. Hover over the "Contribution Tracker →" button. Observe the cursor does not change to a pointer hand. Expected behavior The cursor should change to a pointer (hand icon) when hovering over the button, consistent with all other clickable elements on the page. Actual behavior The cursor stays as the default arrow (cursor: default), making the button appear non-interactive. Environment Page Homepage / Hero section Section "Track Your Contribution Legacy" Element "Contribution Tracker →" CTA button Browser [Please fill in your browser & version] OS [Please fill in your OS] Suggested fix Add the following CSS to the "Contribution Tracker" button's stylesheet: .contribution-tracker-btn { cursor: pointer; } Additional context The adjacent "Node Intelligence" and "Guide" buttons on the same row display the correct pointer cursor on hover. The inconsistency is isolated to the "Contribution Tracker" button only.

issues.title: Bug: No spacing between feature cards on the Features page
issues.body: ### Description The feature cards on the Features page are rendered without any visible spacing (margin/gap) between them, causing them to appear visually cramped and hard to distinguish from one another. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to the Features section of the site 2. Scroll through the feature cards (e.g. "Analyze 30+ File Types", "Generate Markdown Reports", "Collect Static Metrics") 3. Observe that there is no gap or margin between adjacent cards ### Expected Behavior Cards should have consistent spacing between them (e.g. `gap: 1.5rem` or `margin-bottom: 1.5rem`) so they are visually separated and easier to read. ### Actual Behavior Cards are stacked directly against each other with no spacing, making the layout feel cluttered. ### Screenshots _[Attach screenshot here]_ ### Suggested Fix Add a `gap` or `margin` property to the card container or individual card elements in the relevant CSS/Tailwind styles: ```css /* Example fix */ .features-grid { gap: 1.5rem; /* or margin-bottom on each card */ } ``` ### Environment - Browser: [e.g. Chrome 123] - OS: [e.g. macOS / Windows] ### Additional Context This affects the overall readability and visual polish of the Features page.

issues.title: **[BUG] Empty "Copy" button at the bottom of the Quick Start page**
issues.body: ## Description On the [Quick Start — Partial Dependence Functions](https://pygam.readthedocs.io/en/latest/notebooks/quick_start.html) documentation page, there is a code block at the bottom of the page that renders with a **"Copy" button but contains no visible text or code**. The block appears as an empty input field with a "Copy" button — clicking it copies nothing to the clipboard. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to the Quick Start page: https://pygam.readthedocs.io/en/latest/notebooks/quick_start.html 2. Scroll to the very bottom of the page content, just above the "Previous / Next" navigation. 3. Observe an empty code block with a "Copy" button. ## Expected Behavior Either: - The code block should contain the code it is meant to display, OR - The empty block should not be rendered at all. ## Actual Behavior An empty code block is rendered with a functional "Copy" button, but there is no content to copy. ## Screenshots [Attach the two screenshots showing the empty block and the "Copy" button] ## Environment - Documentation site: https://pygam.readthedocs.io (latest) - Observed on: March 26, 2026 - Browser: (add your browser here, e.g. Chrome, Firefox) ## Notes This appears to be a documentation build issue — likely an empty or malformed code cell in the source notebook that gets rendered as a blank block by the Sphinx/nbsphinx pipeline.
